# Questions in Livestream 224

Live Stream #224: Pareidolia Paranoia

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=560" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:20</a> | Will you ever publish your memoirs? | He says no memoirs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=651" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:51</a> | How did the idea of "no Late Bronze Jericho" become consensus? | He says it hardened largely through repeated textbook and scholarly dependence on Kenyon's dating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=805" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:25</a> | How did anti-alcohol faith traditions emerge? | He traces them especially to the gin craze and Methodist temperance movements, not to the biblical world itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=933" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:33</a> | Was the gin craze like what alcohol did to Native Americans? | He says it was similar in having severe social consequences, but not exactly the same. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1028" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:08</a> | Was Djoser the first or second pharaoh of Dynasty 3, and why do books differ? | He says Djoser is second in his chronology, with Nebka first; books differ because early Dynasty 3 placement is debated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1194" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:54</a> | What do you think about getting a doctorate from Melbourne School of Theology? | He says it can be fine if the program fits your need, but advises checking the quality and recognition carefully. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1296" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:36</a> | Why did the Jews keep trying to conquer Jerusalem before David? | He says Jerusalem was a strategic and symbolic stronghold that remained contested until David finally secured it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1386" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:06</a> | Is Jerusalem's strategic value related to why holding Jerusalem has been contentious since 1947? | He says partially yes, because Jerusalem is strategically important for controlling the Judean foothills. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1564" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:04</a> | Were Old Testament manuscripts also written without spaces and punctuation? | Yes. He says the earliest Hebrew texts were primarily consonantal and lacked many later reading aids. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:07</a> | Did final forms of letters like final kaf and final nun already exist? | He says those conventions were later standardizations and were not present in the earliest forms the same way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:49</a> | How could someone read manuscripts written without spaces? | He says readers who knew the language well could still parse the text, even without vowels in some contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1807" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:07</a> | Is the firmament in the Bible a scientific error? | He says no, because the language is describing the world as it appears within an ancient frame of reference. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=1978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:58</a> | What did ancient Jews think the firmament was? | He says they likely thought of it as a real overhead structure that held back the waters above. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=2294" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:14</a> | What do we know about Melchizedek, king of Salem? | He says the text gives only a little: Melchizedek is priest of God Most High and king of Salem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=2396" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:56</a> | What do we know about Salem itself? | He identifies it with Jerusalem and says Middle Bronze fortifications fit the period, though excavation there is difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=2600" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:20</a> | What was Melchizedek's connection to Jesus? | He says Melchizedek is an archetypal priest-king whose pattern points to Christ's non-Aaronic priesthood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=2918" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:38</a> | What do you think of Liverani and his history of Israel? | He is not a fan and says Liverani pushes some odd reconstructions, including the David-warlord angle. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=3670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:10</a> | Why do some translations of John 12:3 say a pound and others say 12 ounces? | He says the issue is simple unit conversion and translation preference, since a Roman pound comes out near 12 ounces. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4065" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:45</a> | What breed is the bunny? | He says she is a Harlequin Lionhead rabbit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:10</a> | What do you think about red-eyed white bunnies? | He says he has no problem with them and cares more about breed and personality. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4260" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:00</a> | Do you ever take your bunnies outside? | He says no, except for necessary vet trips; he thinks rabbits are best kept indoors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4274" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:14</a> | Are they indoor pets only? | He says indoor is best and he would not leave a rabbit in an outdoor hutch. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4301" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:41</a> | Would she bolt the moment the door was open? | He says not typically, unless a rabbit is extremely frightened, but common-sense precautions matter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4404" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:24</a> | Have you ever considered getting birds? | He says no and that he strongly dislikes birds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4750" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:10</a> | Is the serpent in the garden just a normal creature, or a spirit being? | He says it is not a normal creature but a spirit being portrayed with serpent imagery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=4831" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:31</a> | Did dragons akin to St. George's dragon actually exist? | He treats St. George's dragon as an artistic symbol of paganism rather than a literal serpent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=5092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:52</a> | Why could Khayan not be Joseph's pharaoh? | He says Khayan does not fit the chronology he argues for. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=5221" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:01</a> | Was anything interesting happening in Egypt in 2025 BC? | He places it during the reign of Senwosret I in Dynasty 12, but says he does not know of anything especially startling about that date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=5290" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:10</a> | Any insight on Judas hanging versus Acts saying he fell headlong? | He says Acts 1:18-19 should be treated cautiously because he regards that material as textually uncertain; harmonization is possible but not necessary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=5761" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:01</a> | How should we understand Colossians 1:15, especially "firstborn"? | He says "firstborn" there is about rank and preeminence, not Christ being a created being. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=5970" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:30</a> | Does "firstborn from the dead" in Colossians 1:18 have the same idea? | He says it uses the same word and again carries the idea of primacy or first place. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=6637" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:37</a> | What artifact would you want to buy if money were not an object? | He says Amaterasu, the Japanese sword of the sun, though politics would make that impossible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=6990" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:30</a> | What are your thoughts on European rapiers? | He likes them, but says high-quality vintage art rapiers are harder to obtain than good Japanese blades. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=7239" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:39</a> | Is Leviathan a real animal or dinosaur, or a spirit critter? | He says Leviathan is a spirit critter and chaos monster, not a dinosaur, though it may be tied to natural forces. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=7611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:51</a> | Is Jesus the ultimate and final sacrificing converter? | He says Jesus is the ultimate and final sacrifice, but not a sacrificial converter in that specific sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=7738" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:58</a> | What do you think of Expedition Bible's claim that the Pishon is Wadi Rumma? | He says the identification is possible and not original to Expedition Bible, while cautioning that he often finds their material weak. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=7846" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:46</a> | What advice do you have for studying new topics and discerning facts amid bias? | He recommends reading broadly, comparing arguments, and paying attention to the primary evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=8100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:00</a> | How does consensus actually change in history and archaeology? | He says it changes slowly through evidence, argument, and generational turnover rather than by one dramatic reversal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=8248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:28</a> | What is the difference between a hegemony and an empire? | He says an empire directly controls territory, while a hegemony exerts political influence without the same direct control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=8538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:18</a> | Were humanity's earliest encounters with spiritual beings actual interactions, cultural interpretations, or manifestations of consciousness? | He leans toward real spiritual encounters filtered through human interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=9027" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:27</a> | Is Splinter of the Mind's Eye canon? | He says no, though he thinks it is a very good book and wishes it were canon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=9409" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:49</a> | Did you work on a movie at Disney? | He says no; at Disney he worked as a systems performance engineer on the Go.com search engine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=9464" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:44</a> | Why are you so tired? | He says he had been up all night and had not slept. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=9826" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:46</a> | Was the Powerpuff Girls DVD incident during the interactive DVD craze? | He says yes, it was one of those interactive DVDs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=10030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:10</a> | How did they figure out the Powerpuff Girls DVD was infected? | He says a child's computer was scrambled, revealing the problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=10873" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:13</a> | Will AI cripple people's minds? | He says the dumbing down began before AI, especially through grade inflation and declining literacy standards. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=10945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:25</a> | What is a 5.0 GPA? | He treats it as part of grade inflation and says earlier systems topped out at 4.0. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=11897" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:17</a> | Are people really saying AI is the mark of the beast? | He says some people do say that, but he rejects the claim and treats AI as another technology people will have to negotiate with. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=12281" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:41</a> | Did you work for the Department of Defense? | He says no; he worked for a consultant that did DoD work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=12480" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:00</a> | Have you worked with the 8088 processor? | Yes. He says he worked with the 8088, 8086, and older Intel hardware. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/JI6KgCW5B3w?t=12536" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:56</a> | What search engine do you use today, and why? | He says he still uses Google and does not like Microsoft's search engine. |
